name: big-AGI
tagline: A self-hostable, multi-model AI chat workspace you run with your own API keys
seoTitle: big-AGI: Self-Hosted Multi-Model AI Chat UI
metaDescription: big-AGI is an open-source, self-hostable AI chat app. Run many models side by side with Beam, personas, voice, and image generation using your own API keys.
overview: big-AGI is an open-source chat interface for large language models that you host yourself. Instead of locking you into one provider, it connects to many model services through your own API keys, so you can switch between or compare models from different vendors in one place.

It is aimed at developers, researchers, and teams who want more control over their AI tooling than a hosted chat product gives them. Because it is local-first and self-hostable, your conversations and keys stay on infrastructure you choose, and there is no per-message charge from the app itself.

As a chat UI, it sits in the same space as other web front-ends for LLMs, but leans on multi-model workflows: its Beam feature runs a prompt across several models at once so you can compare answers, alongside personas, voice, and image generation.
features: Beam: run one prompt across multiple models at once and compare or merge the results
Connects to 20+ model services using your own API keys, with no vendor lock-in
Personas to shape the assistant's behavior for different tasks
Voice input/output and image generation built into the chat
Self-hostable via Docker or a local Node.js dev server, or deployable to Vercel
Local-first design that keeps your keys and data on infrastructure you control

gettingStarted.intro: You can run big-AGI locally for development or self-host it with Docker. Either way you supply your own model API keys after it starts.

steps.heading: Run with Docker
steps.body: Pull and run the pre-built image, then open http://localhost:3000 in your browser.

steps.heading: Or run locally for development
steps.body: Clone the repo, install dependencies, and start the dev server. The app runs at http://localhost:3000.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/enricoros/big-AGI.git
cd big-AGI
npm install
npm run dev

steps.heading: Add your API keys
steps.body: Open the running app, go to the settings/models panel, and paste in API keys for the model services you want to use. The app does not charge for model usage.

useCases: Compare answers from several models side by side with Beam before trusting a result
Self-host a private AI chat for a team using shared, organization-owned API keys
Experiment with many model providers from one UI without separate accounts or apps
Build voice and image-generation workflows on top of a single chat front-end
